title: Today You Are A Man

jokes.text: My trio of popcorns
jokes.punchline: Pretentious way to describe popcorn containers
jokes.explanation: Silly pretentious language for mundane items - quick economical laugh

jokes.text: I dropped my cell phone into the penguin exhibit... I had my birthday at the Aquarium
jokes.setup: Agent explaining how he lost contacts
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetic detail about celebrating birthday alone at aquarium
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - reveals Simon's patheticness through specific absurd circumstance

jokes.text: I represent the turtle that Tuck is based on
jokes.setup: Simon claiming to be king of renegotiation
jokes.punchline: His example is representing a children's show turtle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Simon's delusions - specific absurd detail that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: Didn't you notice your food wasn't pre-chewed?
jokes.punchline: Reveals bizarrely subservient duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdism - reveals his extreme servitude through disgusting specific detail

jokes.text: I bet he's going to tell me I can't write off all my shoplifting
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to tax fraud through shoplifting writeoffs
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy casual criminality - character-specific absurdism

jokes.text: Getting paid to help a boy become a man is kind of my wheelhouse
jokes.punchline: Jenna's comment has disturbing sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through Jenna's oblivious inappropriate comment

jokes.text: morale-boosting T-shirts indicating everyone 'survived' a certain barbecue
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions deadly microwave weapons through corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate euphemism for mass death - perfectly captures Jack's amoral business mindset

jokes.text: when was the last time I said, 'no, senator, you're out of order'?
jokes.punchline: Specific power fantasy about confronting senators
jokes.explanation: Jack's grandiose self-image revealed through specific fantasy scenario

jokes.text: I would like a hospital bed in my office. A real one. I don't care if it's against the law to resell them
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific request revealing her laziness and legal indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz detail - combines laziness with casual illegality in specific absurd request

jokes.text: And by your 'assistant,' you mean you with a British accent?
jokes.setup: Liz claiming to have new assistant
jokes.punchline: Jack calls out her obvious deception
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character trait with efficient delivery

jokes.text: She's a cool college student from... South Africa. Yeah, she'll be British
jokes.setup: Liz trying to maintain lie about assistant
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence realizes geographic error and commits to accent anyway
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Liz's bad lying and commitment to the bit

jokes.text: You were in the kitchen all day. Right over... Oh, that's a broom
jokes.setup: Tracy insisting Kenneth was there yesterday
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that he was looking at cleaning equipment
jokes.explanation: Strong visual gag with perfect timing - shows how invisible Kenneth is

jokes.text: I am using my once-in-a-lifetime interruption
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only allowed one interruption ever
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth detail - perfectly captures his extreme deference through absurd rule

jokes.text: when a dog goes missing, everyone's upset 'cause there's no dog milk for the babies
jokes.setup: Kenneth comparing himself to a dog
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of dogs and milk
jokes.explanation: Peak Kenneth absurdism - innocent misunderstanding of basic biology creates surreal moment

jokes.text: when 20 cult members jump off a bridge together wearing 'this smells' T-shirts, I should get a cut of that sale
jokes.punchline: Casually describes mass suicide as business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treats mass tragedy as mundane business concern

jokes.text: Yes, many of our viewers are obese
jokes.setup: Host mentions Adam is huge fan
jokes.punchline: Jenna misinterprets and insults their audience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna obliviousness - misunderstands compliment and creates awkwardness

jokes.text: Don't you represent Gina Gershon? My Nemesis? My Nemesis?
jokes.punchline: Jenna's dramatic repetition of nemesis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's melodrama

jokes.text: I was being sarcastic. God, I told mom I wanted Transformer's theme for Bar Mitzvah
jokes.setup: Adam being introduced as huge fan
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hates them and wanted robot theme instead
jokes.explanation: Good setup for episode conflict - bratty kid character establishment

jokes.text: maybe the IRS finds out that your nine dependents are just vibrators
jokes.setup: Marty threatening to expose tax fraud
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about Tracy's fake dependents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail - combines tax fraud with sex toys in specific unexpected way

jokes.text: your 'charity' is just a front that has done nothing to make this country safer from Godzilla attacks. If anything, I've increased the likelihood
jokes.punchline: Tracy's charity is for impossible threat and he's made it worse
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism - completely insane charity concept with perfect escalation

jokes.text: you may recognize me from one of my two background acting gigs
jokes.punchline: Reveals minimal acting experience
jokes.explanation: Standard aspiring actor joke - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Just like my hero, Ivan Drago, from Rocky IV
jokes.punchline: Cites movie villain as inspiration
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - shows Hazel's confused worldview

jokes.text: when your kid throws a tantrum and holds his breath, you hold your breath too. When you regain consciousness, believe me, he's ready to leave the toy store
jokes.setup: Jack giving parenting advice about tantrums
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards logic leading to mutual unconsciousness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack pseudo-wisdom - completely wrong advice delivered with confidence

jokes.text: gross means income before expenses and not gross like apples on a sandwich
jokes.punchline: Reveals her disgusting food preferences
jokes.explanation: Good Liz food grossness - reveals character through mundane clarification

jokes.text: Thank Obama
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning salary decrease for deflation
jokes.punchline: Blames Obama for deflation sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Weak topical reference - doesn't add much beyond political snark

jokes.text: tatertotfreak@hotmail.Com
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing personal email
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz detail - combines food obsession with unprofessionalism

jokes.text: Tell him the funmeister says, 'hi.' He'll know what that means
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Liz is being coached by Pat Riley
jokes.punchline: References embarrassing nickname and mysterious shared history
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - implies embarrassing Jack backstory through specific detail

jokes.text: Do you vacation together on nonjudgmental cruise lines?
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about cruise lines
jokes.explanation: Good weird specific detail - implies cruises are inherently judgmental

jokes.text: My church requires a 110% tithe
jokes.punchline: Mathematically impossible church requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdism - impossible math that explains his poverty

jokes.text: Are you talking about saving squirrels from hawks?
jokes.punchline: Kenneth completely misunderstands concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth innocence - literal interpretation reveals pure worldview

jokes.text: Hair movement... Is a sign of weakness
jokes.setup: Jack giving negotiation advice about appearance
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific detail about hair as weakness
jokes.explanation: Great Jack corporate absurdism - treats natural hair movement as strategic flaw

jokes.text: 90% of negotiations are lost by the person who speaks first
jokes.punchline: Sets up the silence competition that follows
jokes.explanation: Good setup for physical comedy sequence - establishes absurd rule

jokes.text: Because what is speaking a sign of? Weakness? You, out. Fired
jokes.punchline: Someone answers and gets immediately fired for speaking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Jack's rule immediately backfires in absurd way

jokes.text: Yes, may I please speak to pizza?
jokes.setup: Liz making fake emergency phone call
jokes.punchline: Doesn't even try to make fake call believable
jokes.explanation: Good Liz incompetence - even her deception attempts are clumsy

jokes.text: Jack Donaghy... Playing with himself. It's a Jack-off
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jack negotiating against himself
jokes.punchline: Liz delivers obvious masturbation pun
jokes.explanation: Weak obvious pun - sets up perfectly but payoff is predictable

jokes.text: That's the phrase I couldn't remember
jokes.punchline: Tracy admits he forgot the classic routine they were attempting
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment explaining their earlier bad performance

jokes.text: We make other people wait. Forcing people to wait, throwing things, making crazy demands and never being satisfied
jokes.punchline: Accurately describes celebrity behavior as bizarre
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware character moment - Jenna recognizes her own toxicity

jokes.text: Why is the government allowing this?
jokes.setup: Tracy confused about waiting for non-celebrity
jokes.punchline: Thinks government should intervene in social hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy ego moment - expects government protection of celebrity status

jokes.text: A Jewish star
jokes.setup: Jenna saying Adam is the star today
jokes.punchline: Adds religious qualifier for Bar Mitzvah
jokes.explanation: Weak pun - obvious wordplay without much creativity

jokes.text: my house isn't a church, although I do let children drink wine there
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits to giving alcohol to minors
jokes.explanation: Good dark Tracy moment - accidentally confesses to crime while defending himself

jokes.text: Like all actors, I'm not emotionally mature enough to deal with my feelings, you fart-face
jokes.setup: Jenna giving psychological insight about actors
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves point with childish insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor - Jenna demonstrates the exact immaturity she describes

jokes.text: I know you are, but what am I?
jokes.punchline: Tracy responds with even more childish comeback
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of childishness - proves Jenna's point about actors

jokes.text: This is the men's room, Jenna. Not the C-list skank's room
jokes.setup: Jenna entering bathroom to talk to Adam
jokes.punchline: Kid delivers harsh celebrity ranking insult
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - kid's brutal honesty about Jenna's career status

jokes.text: Not Napoleon, not Hitler, not even Balki in the unaired ninth season of Perfect strangers
jokes.setup: Jack explaining no one wins land wars in Russia
jokes.punchline: Escalates from historical figures to obscure sitcom character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - mixes real history with absurd fake TV reference

jokes.text: I've played this Japanese video game where you slap prostitutes to death, but you only ever dance with this penguin named Yamagiku.
jokes.setup: Adam explaining his experience with girls
jokes.punchline: Violent game experience vs. innocent dancing with penguin
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - juxtaposition of violence with innocent dancing

jokes.text: You're rich. And you're fat, so you'll die young. Women love that
jokes.setup: Jenna reassuring Adam about attractiveness to women
jokes.punchline: Lists wealth and early death as romantic assets
jokes.explanation: Dark Jenna wisdom - treats morbid reality as romantic advantage

jokes.text: Put money in the girl's mouth. Also, my friend, Darryl, is your real father
jokes.setup: Tracy giving dance advice to Adam
jokes.punchline: Bizarre advice followed by random paternity revelation
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism - combines bad advice with random shocking disclosure

jokes.text: Did you put peanuts on this? Yeah, it's delicious, hat guy. Ah... I'm allergic!
jokes.punchline: Gives him allergens despite him asking about it
jokes.explanation: Standard incompetent employee gag - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I wish I was dead!
jokes.setup: Toofer having allergic reaction and work stress
jokes.punchline: Overdramatic death wish from minor problems
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation - overdramatic response without much craft

jokes.text: When is that party you throw that we never go to 'cause we don't know what to get the man who has nothing
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to prove he knows Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Reveals they ignore Kenneth's parties due to his poverty
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - reveals casual cruelty disguised as gift-giving dilemma

jokes.text: Like the polio vaccine. Or a no-bottom strip club near a Wendy's
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing honest communication to good things
jokes.punchline: Equates medical breakthrough with bizarre sexual venue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy comparison - mixes genuine good with absurd specific fantasy

jokes.text: I'm not wearing stockings. That's a vein
jokes.setup: Jack pointing out run in stocking
jokes.punchline: Liz reveals it's her actual varicose vein
jokes.explanation: Great Liz gross-out moment - reveals physical deterioration through negotiation mistake

jokes.text: you have to say, 'go for Liz,' when you answer your phone
jokes.punchline: Forces Liz to use embarrassing phone greeting
jokes.explanation: Good humiliation tactic - forces specific embarrassing behavior

jokes.text: I'm just doing the sorcerer's apprentice. You respond with a pirate holiday, and I have no choice but to play the hillbilly auction
jokes.setup: Jack explaining negotiation is taking too long
jokes.punchline: References nonsensical negotiation strategies with fantasy names
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack corporate speak - creates entirely fictional negotiation terminology

jokes.text: It'll be back to submitting topical menopause jokes to joy behar by fax
jokes.punchline: Describes pathetically specific career rock bottom
jokes.explanation: Great specific nightmare scenario - combines outdated technology with niche comedy

jokes.text: You'd have more lawyers on you than a midtown hooker
jokes.setup: Liz threatening Jack with legal consequences
jokes.punchline: Uses bizarrely specific prostitute comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent Liz attempt at tough talk with inappropriate metaphor

jokes.text: Then an elegante, parry with an elegante primo, 5%, I demand three years... No, that can't be right. Elegante, elegante primo, carry the one...
jokes.punchline: Treats negotiation like mathematical fencing with made-up terms
jokes.explanation: Great Jack corporate absurdism - creates elaborate fantasy system for simple negotiation

jokes.text: Good God. You won. I did? You got everything you wanted. Kabletown Jack made a mistake, and Lemon Jack pounced on it. I-me lost
jokes.punchline: Jack refers to himself in third person multiple personalities
jokes.explanation: Good Jack identity crisis - treats his divided loyalties as separate entities

jokes.text: Nobody beats the Liz!
jokes.punchline: Uses sports cliche in awkward third person
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Liz's awkward celebration style

jokes.text: we're going to be more 'haw-naist'
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'honest' in exaggerated way
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy speech pattern - shows education gaps through pronunciation

jokes.text: Because I was raised in foster care
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining he's not used to change
jokes.punchline: Reveals traumatic backstory casually
jokes.explanation: Good character development - reveals serious backstory through comedy exercise

jokes.text: Boy, you are dumb sometimes
jokes.punchline: Kenneth delivers rare direct insult
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Kenneth's rare moment of directness surprises

jokes.text: Men wanted to be me, women wanted to sleep with me, bisexuals wanted to watch
jokes.setup: Jack lamenting his fall from power
jokes.punchline: Includes bisexuals in his fantasy appeal taxonomy
jokes.explanation: Good Jack ego moment - escalates standard boast with unexpected third category

jokes.text: Maybe I'll just quit and... Go work for a not-for-profit. Somewhere where there's less pressure and the people are nice. And I can wear sneakers and jeans on Friday
jokes.punchline: His idea of radical change is casual Friday
jokes.explanation: Great Jack character moment - his rebellion is pathetically mild corporate change

jokes.text: I want to be somebody else! I want to be a baby again!
jokes.punchline: Wants to regress to infancy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's breakdown to ultimate regression

jokes.text: No, no, no. Look, you won. Just don't cry. Daddy doesn't cry
jokes.setup: Liz trying to comfort crying Jack
jokes.punchline: Calls herself daddy in weird parental role
jokes.explanation: Good weird Liz moment - awkward gender-confused parental comfort

jokes.text: Have you not read the poetry of jewel?
jokes.punchline: Cites pop singer's poetry as emotional warfare manual
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack moment - treats terrible pop poetry as strategic literature

jokes.text: So that means that my me-I taught your you-you a negotiation trick. Yes, I suppose you did
jokes.punchline: Complicated pronoun confusion with multiple selves
jokes.explanation: Decent verbal confusion gag extending the multiple identity theme

jokes.text: He's a white male with hair, Lemon. The sky's the limit
jokes.punchline: Lists minimal demographics as unlimited potential
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary through Jack's casual acknowledgment of privilege

jokes.text: To white men!
jokes.setup: Jack's comment about white male privilege
jokes.punchline: They toast to demographic advantage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of privilege joke into toast format

jokes.text: Those players have some crazy names. Like Derek and Alex
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming to know Yankees players
jokes.punchline: Lists completely normal names as crazy
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy anti-humor - treats mundane names as exotic

jokes.text: His wife's name is don't. What's the guy's name on first base? Know what died in Vietnam
jokes.punchline: Completely butcher classic comedy bit with nonsense
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing continued incompetence at classic routine

tmdb_overview: Liz finds a secret weapon to help in her contract negotiations with Jack, Kenneth leaves his job because he feels under-appreciated, and Tracy and Jenna perform at their accountant's ungrateful son's Bar Mitzvah.
insight: Bar mitzvah plot gives 30 Rock enough absurdist momentum to nearly crack 80.
